generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance SINUMERIK840DController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller that interacts with the robot to provide position, speed, or acceleration inputs and receive target/actual values (position, velocity, acceleration, and motor current) as feedback. Acts as a data acquisition system streaming time-series data via MQTT protocol."
]

instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor that converts electrical current (I_M) into torque (τ_M) for the first axis of the MABI MAX 100 robot."
]

instance CycloidalGearbox : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Cycloidal gearbox component in the gear transmission system that transmits torque from the motor to the output shaft while reducing speed and increasing torque."
]

instance GearWheel : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Gearwheel component in the gear transmission system that works in conjunction with the cycloidal gearbox to transmit rotational motion from the motor to the output axis."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted on the motor providing angular position data (r_M) to reduce positional uncertainty and enable analysis of elasticity and friction in the drive train."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorEncoderData
]

instance SecondaryEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted behind the gearbox providing angular position data (r_G) to reduce backlash and positional uncertainty in the drive train."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SecondaryEncoderData
]

instance MotorCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Current sensor measuring input current (I_M) to the motor, used to derive torque (τ_M) via manufacturer-provided motor constants."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorCurrentData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance MotorEncoderData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
]

instance SecondaryEncoderData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryEncoder
]

instance MotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentSensor
]

instance AxisPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SINUMERIK840DController
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLStructuralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Block Definition Diagram (BDD) and Internal Block Diagram (IBD) describing the structural composition of the first axis (motor, gearwheel, cycloidal gearbox) and their interconnections (rotational and current flows)."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLTooling
]

instance SysMLBehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML model defining behavioral aspects of the first axis including dynamic relationships between components and friction effects."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLTooling
]

instance TorqueVelocityDerivationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Data calculation model deriving torque and angular velocity from measured motor current (I_M) and encoder data using manufacturer-provided motor constants."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulink
]

instance IdealGearboxModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink model simulating ideal behavior of the gearbox without friction, serving as a baseline for comparison."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulink
]

instance CoulombViscousFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink model implementing Coulomb-Viscous friction model to simulate friction losses in the gearbox."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulink
]

instance StribeckFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink model implementing Stribeck friction model to simulate friction losses with velocity-dependent Stribeck effect."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulink
]

instance DahlFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink model implementing Dahl friction model to simulate hysteresis effects in the gearbox."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulink
]

instance LuGreFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink model implementing LuGre friction model for detailed simulation of friction characteristics."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulink
]

instance MeasuredMotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw measured data trace of motor current (I_M) streamed via MQTT from the SINUMERIK 840D controller."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TorqueVelocityDerivationModel
]

instance MeasuredMotorPositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw measured angular position data (r_M) from the motor encoder streamed via MQTT from the SINUMERIK 840D controller."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorEncoderData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TorqueVelocityDerivationModel
]

instance MeasuredGearboxPositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw measured angular position data (r_G) from the secondary encoder streamed via MQTT from the SINUMERIK 840D controller."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SecondaryEncoderData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TorqueVelocityDerivationModel
]

instance DerivedTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Derived torque values (τ_M) calculated from motor current (I_M) using manufacturer-provided motor constants."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MeasuredMotorCurrentData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CoulombViscousFrictionModel, StribeckFrictionModel, DahlFrictionModel, LuGreFrictionModel
]

instance DerivedAngularVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Derived angular velocity data (ω_M) calculated from motor position data (r_M) and time stamps."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MeasuredMotorPositionData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TorqueVelocityDerivationModel
]

instance SimulatedIdealOutputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated output data (r_out) from the ideal gearbox model without friction for comparison purposes."
    DTDFVocab:fromData IdealGearboxModel
]

instance SimulatedFrictionCompensatedOutputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated output data (r_out) from friction models (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck, Dahl, LuGre) with friction compensation applied."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CoulombViscousFrictionModel, StribeckFrictionModel, DahlFrictionModel, LuGreFrictionModel
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLTooling : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML modeling tooling for creating structural (BDD) and behavioral models of the robot's first axis."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionModelIdentificationService
]

instance MATLABSimulink : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink environment for executing executable friction and dynamics models of the gearbox and motor."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionModelIdentificationService, DataDrivenFrictionCompensationService
]

instance MQTTDataStreaming : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol implementation for real-time streaming of time-series data (I_M, r_M, r_G, r_out) from the SINUMERIK 840D controller to the digital twin."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataAcquisitionService
]

instance DigitalShadowReferenceModel : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Framework for purpose-driven digital shadow design including data trace aggregation, model integration, and traceability of data origins."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionModelIdentificationService, DataDrivenFrictionCompensationService
]

instance AssetAdministrationShell : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Asset Administration Shell providing contextual metadata and system configuration tracking for the digital twin."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService, ModelBasedControlSupportService
]

// C6: Services
instance FrictionModelIdentificationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous, real-time identification of joint friction (static, kinetic, viscous) using white-box (analytical) and black-box (data-driven) models (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck, Dahl, LuGre) to improve motion accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:provides StaticFrictionInsight, KineticFrictionInsight, ViscousFrictionInsight, FrictionModelUpdateAction
]

instance DataDrivenFrictionCompensationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Integration of friction models with live operational data to subtract friction losses from simulated behavior, enabling deviation analysis between measured and simulated data."
    DTDFVocab:provides DeviationAnalysisInsight, FrictionCompensationAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Unified dashboard for displaying simulated vs. measured data (torque τ, angular velocity ω) to enhance understanding of physical relationships in the first axis."
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualAnalyticsInsight
]

instance ModelBasedControlSupportService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides insights for model-based control algorithms by supplying precise dynamics models of the robot, including friction effects."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlAlgorithmTuningInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance StaticFrictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification of static friction components in the gearbox enabling model-based control algorithm tuning."
]

instance KineticFrictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification of kinetic friction components in the gearbox enabling model-based control algorithm tuning."
]

instance ViscousFrictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification of viscous friction components in the gearbox enabling model-based control algorithm tuning."
]

instance DeviationAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantification of discrepancies between simulated (friction-compensated) and measured data, highlighting areas for improvement in the robot's motion accuracy."
]

instance VisualAnalyticsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time monitoring of friction effects and trend analysis over time displayed in the unified visualization dashboard."
]

instance FrictionModelUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Suggests adaptive adjustments to friction models (e.g., Stribeck curve parameters) based on live operational data to mitigate wear or environmental changes."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance FrictionCompensationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Applies friction compensation to simulated behavior using identified friction models to improve motion accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ControlAlgorithmTuningInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Provides precise dynamics models of the robot including friction effects to support tuning of model-based control algorithms."
]

// C18: Environment
instance RobotFirstAxisEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Physical environment containing the first axis components of the MABI MAX 100 robot including motor, gearwheel, cycloidal gearbox, and associated sensors."
    base:contains PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor, CycloidalGearbox, GearWheel, MotorEncoder, SecondaryEncoder, MotorCurrentSensor
]

instance DigitalTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Digital environment containing all digital twin components including models, data, enablers, and services."
    base:contains SysMLStructuralModel, SysMLBehavioralModel, TorqueVelocityDerivationModel, IdealGearboxModel, CoulombViscousFrictionModel, StribeckFrictionModel, DahlFrictionModel, LuGreFrictionModel,
                MeasuredMotorCurrentData, MeasuredMotorPositionData, MeasuredGearboxPositionData, DerivedTorqueData, DerivedAngularVelocityData,
                SimulatedIdealOutputData, SimulatedFrictionCompensatedOutputData,
                SysMLTooling, MATLABSimulink, MQTTDataStreaming, DigitalShadowReferenceModel, AssetAdministrationShell,
                FrictionModelIdentificationService, DataDrivenFrictionCompensationService, VisualizationService, ModelBasedControlSupportService
]

// Added missing services
instance DataAcquisitionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for acquiring and processing time-series data from the physical system via MQTT protocol."
    DTDFVocab:provides MeasuredMotorCurrentData, MeasuredMotorPositionData, MeasuredGearboxPositionData
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The digital twin operates on a **real-time, continuous time scale**, synchronized with the physical system’s operational data. Data is streamed at high frequency (exact rate not specified but implied to be sufficient for motion control) via MQTT, and friction models are updated dynamically during operation. The twinning process aligns with the **operational phase** of the robot, focusing on **real-time monitoring and adaptive modeling** (e.g., accounting for wear, tool changes, or environmental factors like temperature)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The digital twin constellation is **purpose-driven**, centered on **friction modeling and motion accuracy improvement** for the MABI MAX 100’s first axis. The constellation includes:
   - **Physical System (PT)**: MABI MAX 100 robot with sensors (encoders, current sensor) and CNC controller.
   - **Digital Shadow (DS)**: A **one-way data flow** system integrating:
     - **Engineering models** (SysML),
     - **Simulation models** (Simulink),
     - **Data calculation models** (torque/velocity derivation),
     - **Processing components** (consuming input traces, applying friction models, producing output traces).
   - **Data Sources**: MQTT-streamed time-series data (I_M, angles, derived quantities).
   - **Outputs**: Visualized deviations between simulated (friction-compensated) and measured data for **control algorithm refinement**.
   The constellation does not include **bidirectional control loops** but serves as a **diagnostic and analysis tool** for improving robot performance."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The digital twin operates primarily during the **operational phase** of the robot’s life cycle, focusing on **as-operated** representation of the physical twin (PT). It does not cover the **as-designed** (ideal) or **as-manufactured** phases explicitly, though the **engineering models** (SysML) describe the intended structure and behavior. The DS is designed to **continuously improve** during operation by refining friction models based on live data, aligning with **adaptive maintenance and control optimization** goals."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The fidelity and validity of the digital twin are ensured through:
   - **Model Validation**: Simulation models (Simulink) are validated against **measured data traces** (e.g., torque τ, angular velocity ω) to assess accuracy of friction compensation.
   - **Uncertainty Handling**: The DS accounts for **wear, tool changes, and environmental factors** (e.g., temperature) by continuously updating friction models with live data.
   - **Traceability**: Each data trace is linked to its **source (e.g., MQTT channel, sensor)** and **processing component** (e.g., Simulink simulation), ensuring reproducibility and debugging capabilities.
   - **Verification**: The **deviation analysis** between simulated (friction-compensated) and measured data provides a metric for validating model accuracy. No explicit statistical validation methods (e.g., RMSE, confidence intervals) are mentioned, but the **purpose-driven approach** ensures alignment with operational goals."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical connection between the physical twin (PT) and digital twin (DT) involves:
   - **Data Protocols**: **MQTT** for **real-time streaming** of time-series data (e.g., I_M, angles, derived quantities) from the Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller to the DS.
   - **Network Architecture**: The DS operates as a **passive observer**, receiving data unidirectionally from the PT. No return data flow to the PT is implemented.
   - **Data Format**: Time-series data is structured as **arrays of floating-point values** (e.g., torque τ, angular velocity ω) with **metadata** (source, timestamp) for traceability."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The digital twin is deployed in a **centralized environment** integrating:
  - **MQTT Broker**: Hosts the **MQTT protocol** for **real-time data streaming** from the Siemens controller.
  - **Simulink Runtime Environment**: Executes **executable models** (friction, dynamics) for **data processing**.
  - **Dashboard Interface**: Visualizes **deviation analysis** and **friction characteristics** via a **unified UI**.
  - **Model Repository**: Stores **SysML models**, **Simulink simulations**, and **data traces** for **traceability and reproducibility**.
  The exact **physical hosting infrastructure** (e.g., cloud, on-premise) is **not specified** in the documents, though the system is designed for **local deployment** alongside the robot’s CNC controller."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The digital twin integrates horizontally with:
   - **Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller**: Provides **real-time sensor data** (e.g., encoders, current) via MQTT.
   - **Manufacturer-provided motor parameters**: Used in **data calculation models** (e.g., torque derivation) to ensure consistency with the physical system.
   - **Potential extension**: The DS framework could integrate with **other digital twins** (e.g., for other robot axes) or **enterprise systems** (e.g., MES, ERP) for broader manufacturing analytics, though this is not implemented in the current use case."
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "The documents do not explicitly address data ownership or privacy. No information is provided regarding:
  - Whether the **PT owner** or **DT service provider** holds ownership of the **sensor data** or **model outputs**.
  - Measures for **data encryption**, **access control**, or **personal data protection**.
  - Ethical considerations for **data sharing** or **third-party integration**.
  The DS focuses on **technical implementation** without addressing **legal or privacy frameworks**."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The digital twin adheres to the following standards and frameworks:
   - **SysML (Systems Modeling Language)**: For **engineering models** (structural and behavioral descriptions).
   - **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)**: Part of the **Industry 4.0 reference architecture** for **metadata and contextual information**.
   - **MQTT (Message Queuing Telemetry Transport)**: Industry-standard protocol for **real-time data streaming** in IoT and industrial applications.
   - **Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM)**: A **domain-specific reference model** for DS design, conforming to **model-driven engineering principles**.
   - **MATLAB Simulink**: Industry-standard tool for **dynamics and control system simulation**."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The digital twin incorporates the following security and safety measures:
  1. **Passive Operation**: The DS is a **read-only entity**; it **does not execute commands** on the PT, reducing **cybersecurity risks** associated with remote control.
  2. **Physical Safety Constraints**: The robot’s **safety cell** restricts the **working range to [-90°, 90°]** and **maximum speed (75°/s)**, mitigating **collision risks**.
  3. **Data Integrity**: **MQTT streaming** ensures **real-time synchronization** between PT and DS, but **no explicit encryption or authentication protocols** are mentioned.
  4. **Industrial Safety**: Compliance with **ISO 13849** (safety-related parts of control systems) is **implied** via the **Siemens CNC controller**, though not explicitly stated.
  The DS **does not support remote PT operations**; its role is **diagnostic and analytical**, not control-centric."
]
